Notice Of Motin For An Order Compelling The Turning Over Of Certain Records Fo The Elite Cafe, Inc.
Off Calendar Jul-10-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Morgan Jones for plaintiff and moving party Miranda William

NO OTHER APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Miranda Williams Motion to compel granted, as follows:
1. Defendant Clendening shall produce documents in the possession, custody or control of defendant Clendening individually or in his capacity as officer or director of defendant Elite Cafe;
2. The documents to be produced shall set forth the names, addresses, telephone numbers and/or other contact information for individuals employed by Elite Cafe at any time during the period November 1, 2004 through April 30, 2005;
3. So long as the documents produced reveal employee names, addresses, telephone numbers, and/or other such contact information, the documents produced may be redacted as necessary to protect the privacy rights of the individual employees;
4. Request for sanctions is denied. Commissioner: PAUL SLAVIT

RULING; Argued and the court TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION.MOVING PARTY TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER. 391 COMPLIANCE REQUIRED.(302/REQ/PB)TINA PAXTON #1558

Mtnto Quash Subpoena Duces Tecum Of Employment Records; Req For Judicial Ntc
Off Calendar Nov-18-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff's motion to quash employment records subpoenas is granted in part. As drafted, the subpoenas are overbroad. The Court modifies the subpoenas to only require production of records indicating the length of employment, job titles, job descriptions, salary earned for each position held, and payroll records, including salary, wages, commissions or other remuneration paid or held by the employer, earning statements, paychecks, W-2 and / or 1099 forms, and employment benefit packages. All requests for monetary sanctions are denied. Prevailing party to submit form of order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
